# Command reference

<!-- GENERATED by `cargo xtask gen-docs` from the mtui command registry. Do not edit by hand; re-run the task to regenerate. -->

Every command below is available in the `mtui` REPL (see [Invocation](invocation.md) for how to start it and load an update). Commands not on the [MCP deny-list](mcp.md) are also exposed as `mtui-mcp` tools. Each command additionally accepts the shared template flags `-T/--template <RRID>` and `--all-templates`; the cross-command behaviour behind them (fan-out, host-pool selection, locking) is described in [Workflow concepts](concepts.md).

## `run`

Runs a command on a specified host or on all enabled targets.

```text
Usage: run [OPTIONS] [COMMAND]...

Arguments:
  [COMMAND]...
          Command to run on refhost

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `lrun`

Runs a command in the local shell.

```text
Usage: lrun [COMMAND]...

Arguments:
  [COMMAND]...
          command to run on local shell

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `update`

Applies the testing update to the target hosts.

```text
Usage: update [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

      --newpackage
          Install new packages after update

      --noprepare
          Skip prepare procedure

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `install`

Installs packages from the current active repositories.

```text
Usage: install [OPTIONS] <PACKAGE>...

Arguments:
  <PACKAGE>...
          package to install

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `uninstall`

Removes packages from the system.

```text
Usage: uninstall [OPTIONS] <PACKAGE>...

Arguments:
  <PACKAGE>...
          package to remove

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `prepare`

Installs missing packages and updates existing packages.

```text
Usage: prepare [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -f, --force
          force package installation

  -i, --installed
          prepare only installed packages

  -u, --update
          enable test update repositories

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `downgrade`

Downgrades all related packages to the last released version.

```text
Usage: downgrade [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `reboot`

Reboots reference hosts and reconnects once they are back up.

```text
Usage: reboot [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `set_repo`

Adds or removes an issue repository to or from hosts.

```text
Usage: set_repo [OPTIONS] <--add|--remove>

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -A, --add
          Add issue repos to refhosts

  -R, --remove
          Remove issue repos from refhosts

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `show_update_repos`

Shows the update repositories that are valid for the current update.

```text
Usage: show_update_repos

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `add_host`

Adds one or more reference hosts to the target host list.

```text
Usage: add_host [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Address of the target host (FQDN). Can be repeated

  -k, --keep-mode
          Do not switch to the manual workflow when in automatic mode

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `remove_host`

Disconnects from a host and removes it from the list.

```text
Usage: remove_host [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `set_host_state`

Sets the state and execution mode of a host.

```text
Usage: set_host_state [OPTIONS] <state>

Arguments:
  <state>
          enabled | disabled | dryrun | parallel | serial
          
          [possible values: parallel, serial, dryrun, disabled, enabled]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `lock`

Locks hosts for exclusive usage (the operation/zypper lock).

```text
Usage: lock [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -c, --comment <COMMENT>...
          Lock comment (keeps the lock effective across sessions)

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `unlock`

Unlocks hosts previously locked with `lock`.

```text
Usage: unlock [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -f, --force
          Force unlock - remove locks set by other users or sessions

  -p, --pool
          Remove the pool claim instead of the zypper/operation lock

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `switch`

Switches the active template to another loaded one.

```text
Usage: switch <RRID>

Arguments:
  <RRID>
          RRID of the loaded template to make active

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `unload`

Unloads one loaded template, closing only its host connections.

```text
Usage: unload <RRID>

Arguments:
  <RRID>
          RRID of the loaded template to unload

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `list_templates`

Lists all loaded templates, marking the active one.

```text
Usage: list_templates

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `whoami`

Displays the current user name and session PID.

```text
Usage: whoami

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `list_products`

Prints the installed products on the reference hosts.

```text
Usage: list_products [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `reload_products`

Reloads and re-parses the products on the target reference hosts.

```text
Usage: reload_products [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `config`

Shows or sets runtime configuration values.

```text
Usage: config <COMMAND>

Commands:
  show  
  set   
  help  Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `quit`

*Aliases:* `exit`, `EOF`

Disconnect from all hosts and exit (optionally reboot/poweroff).

```text
Usage: quit [BOOTARG]

Arguments:
  [BOOTARG]
          reboot or poweroff refhosts on exit
          
          [possible values: reboot, poweroff]

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `shell`

Opens an interactive shell on a reference host.

```text
Usage: shell [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `checkout`

Updates the loaded template's files from SVN (`svn up`).

```text
Usage: checkout

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `commit`

Commits the testing template working copy to SVN.

```text
Usage: commit [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -m, --msg <MSG>...
          commit message

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `show_diff`

Shows the raw OBS source diff.

```text
Usage: show_diff

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `analyze_diff`

Analyzes the OBS source diff to assist code review.

```text
Usage: analyze_diff

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `list_bugs`

Lists related bugs and their Bugzilla/Jira URLs.

```text
Usage: list_bugs

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `list_metadata`

Lists the patchinfo metadata for the loaded test report.

```text
Usage: list_metadata

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `list_hosts`

Lists all connected hosts with their system, state, and execution mode.

```text
Usage: list_hosts

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `list_timeout`

Prints the current command timeout per host, in seconds.

```text
Usage: list_timeout

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `list_update_commands`

Lists the commands mtui would invoke to apply the update on the hosts.

```text
Usage: list_update_commands

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `list_sessions`

Lists the active SSH sessions connected to each reference host.

```text
Usage: list_sessions [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `list_locks`

Lists the lock state of all connected hosts.

```text
Usage: list_locks [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -p, --pool
          list pool-claim locks instead of zypper/operation locks

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `list_history`

Lists the history of mtui events recorded on the reference hosts.

```text
Usage: list_history [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -e, --event <event>
          event type to list (repeatable)
          
          [possible values: connect, disconnect, install, update, downgrade]

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `show_log`

Prints the command protocol (issued commands + output) from the hosts.

```text
Usage: show_log [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `list_versions`

Lists the available versions of packages in the enabled repositories.

```text
Usage: list_versions [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -p, --package <PACKAGE>
          package name to show versions for (repeatable)

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `list_packages`

Lists packages and their installed versions on the reference hosts.

```text
Usage: list_packages [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -p, --package <PACKAGE>
          package name to list (repeatable)

  -w, --wanted
          print versions wanted by the testreport

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `set_timeout`

Sets the command-execution timeout, in seconds, for the selected hosts.

```text
Usage: set_timeout [OPTIONS] <timeout>

Arguments:
  <timeout>
          Timeout in seconds; "0" disables it

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `put`

Uploads a local file (or directory tree) to every enabled reference host.

```text
Usage: put <filename>

Arguments:
  <filename>
          file (or directory) to upload to all hosts

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `get`

Downloads a remote file from every enabled reference host.

```text
Usage: get <filename>

Arguments:
  <filename>
          file to download from the target hosts

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `checkers`

Lists the build-check (checker) result runs for the loaded update.

```text
Usage: checkers

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `updates`

Lists the update queue (unassigned + in-testing by default), fetched live from the TeReGen API.

```text
Usage: updates [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --review-group <GROUP>
          filter by review group as the bare group name, e.g. qam-sle (not the '<group>-review' login form, which classic rows lack)

      --status <STATUS>
          filter by status (default: testing); use 'all' for every status
          
          [default: testing]

      --limit <N>
          cap the number of rows (0 = all)
          
          [default: 0]

      --assignee <USER>
          filter to updates assigned to this user (any qam group)

      --mine
          filter to updates assigned to the current session user

      --all-assignees
          show every update regardless of assignee, overriding the unassigned default

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `openqa_overview`

Prints an openQA / QAM Dashboard / build-checks overview for the loaded MU.

```text
Usage: openqa_overview [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --no-aggregated
          Skip the Aggregated Updates section

      --days <N>
          How many days to scan back for aggregated builds (1-30, default 5)
          
          [default: 5]

      --aggregated-groups <GROUP>
          Job groups to search in Aggregated Updates (default: core)
          
          [possible values: core, containers, yast, security]

      --url-openqa <URL>
          Override openQA URL (default: config openqa_instance)

      --url-dashboard-qam <URL>
          Override QAM Dashboard base URL (default: derived from config)

      --url-qam <URL>
          Override QAM base URL (default: derived from config reports_url)

      --test-pattern <REGEX>
          Custom regex to extract test results from build-check logs

      --export
          Also inject the overview into the loaded testreport's log

      --no-fetch
          (deferred) reuse cached overview; currently fetches anyway

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `openqa_jobs`

Lists the individual openQA jobs for the loaded update's incident build.

```text
Usage: openqa_jobs [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --all
          keep obsoleted jobs (superseded by a retrigger)

      --failed
          show only genuinely failed jobs (pending/unfinished excluded)

      --arch <ARCH>
          only jobs for this architecture

      --url-openqa <URL>
          Override openQA URL (default: config openqa_instance)

      --url-dashboard-qam <URL>
          Override QAM Dashboard base URL (default: derived from config)

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `reload_openqa`

Reloads information from the openQA instances.

```text
Usage: reload_openqa

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `set_workflow`

Sets the workflow and reloads data from openQA.

```text
Usage: set_workflow <workflow>

Arguments:
  <workflow>
          desired workflow - auto, manual or kernel
          
          [possible values: auto, manual, kernel]

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `set_log_level`

Changes the current mtui log level.

```text
Usage: set_log_level <level>

Arguments:
  <level>
          log level for mtui - info, warning, error or debug
          
          [possible values: info, warning, error, debug]

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `assign`

Assigns a review request to a user or group.

```text
Usage: assign [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -g, --group <GROUP>
          Group to act on (not valid for the Gitea workflow)

  -u, --user <USER>
          User override for the Gitea workflow (Gitea only)
          
          [default: ""]

  -f, --force
          Force assign the review in Gitea even without an open group

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `unassign`

Unassigns a review request.

```text
Usage: unassign [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -g, --group <GROUP>
          Group to act on (not valid for the Gitea workflow)

  -u, --user <USER>
          User override for the Gitea workflow (Gitea only)
          
          [default: ""]

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `reject`

Rejects a review request.

```text
Usage: reject [OPTIONS] --reason <reason>

Options:
  -g, --group <GROUP>
          Group to act on (not valid for the Gitea workflow)

  -u, --user <USER>
          User override for the Gitea workflow (Gitea only)
          
          [default: ""]

  -r, --reason <reason>
          Reason to reject the update (required)
          
          [possible values: admin, retracted, build_problem, not_fixed, regression, false_reject, tracking_issue]

  -m, --message [<message>...]
          Rejection message (takes the remainder of the command)

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `comment`

Adds a comment to a review request.

```text
Usage: comment [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -m, --message <message>...
          The comment body (required; interactive prompt lands in Phase 6)

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `approve`

Approves the loaded update, dispatching to OSC or Gitea like the other backend-API commands.

```text
Usage: approve [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -g, --group <GROUP>
          Group to approve (not valid for the Gitea workflow)

  -u, --user <USER>
          User override for the Gitea workflow (Gitea only)
          
          [default: ""]

  -r, --reviewer <NAME>
          Record reviewer in the testreport, commit to SVN, then approve

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `regenerate`

Regenerates a test-report template via the TeReGen API (a given RRID need not be loaded first).

```text
Usage: regenerate [OPTIONS] [RRID]

Arguments:
  [RRID]
          template to regenerate even if not loaded (default: the loaded template)

Options:
      --force
          overwrite an existing (but unedited) template

      --ignore-inconsistent
          regenerate despite inconsistent metadata (e.g. arch mismatch)

      --no-wait
          enqueue the job and return without waiting or reloading

  -k, --kernel
          load the standalone RRID as a kernel update (default: auto)

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `request_review`

Requests review of the loaded update in Slack.

```text
Usage: request_review [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -c, --channel <CHANNEL>
          Channel to post to, overriding the configured one

  -m, --message <TEXT>
          Extra context appended to the review request

  -w, --watch
          After posting, watch the message for reviewer reactions until a verdict or timeout (Ctrl-C stops it). Over MCP, pair this with background=true so the call does not outlive the client timeout

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `export`

Exports the gathered update data to the testing template.

```text
Usage: export [OPTIONS] [FILENAME]

Arguments:
  [FILENAME]
          output template file name (defaults to the loaded template)

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -f, --force
          force overwrite existing template and re-download openQA results present in the log

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `list_refhosts`

Lists reference hosts from `refhosts.yml` (offline search, no connect).

```text
Usage: list_refhosts [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -T, --testplatform <QUERY>
          match a SMELT testplatform query

  -n, --name <GLOB>
          hostname glob, e.g. 'whale-*' or '*.qam.suse.cz'

  -a, --arch <ARCH>
          arch filter (repeatable): x86_64 / aarch64 / ppc64le / s390x

  -p, --product <SUBSTR>
          base-product substring, e.g. sles / sled / SLE_HPC

      --version <VERSION>
          product version: 15-SP6 / 15.6 / 15 (SP optional)

      --addon <SUBSTR>
          addon-name substring (repeatable)

      --pool
          group by test-target slot (product+version+arch+addons)

      --json
          emit JSON

      --free
          also probe live mtui-lock state (connects to each matched host)

  -v, --verbose
          include addons in the output

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `load_template`

Loads a maintenance-update template into the session and connects its reference hosts.

```text
Usage: load_template <--auto-review-id <RequestReviewID>|--kernel-review-id <RequestReviewID>>

Options:
  -a, --auto-review-id <RequestReviewID>
          OBS request review id, e.g. SUSE:Maintenance:1:1

  -k, --kernel-review-id <RequestReviewID>
          OBS kernel/live-patch request review id, e.g. SUSE:Maintenance:1:1

  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `help`

List commands, or show help for one command.

```text
Usage: help [COMMAND]

Arguments:
  [COMMAND]
          command to show help for; omit to list all commands

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `edit`

Edit the active testing template or a local file in $EDITOR.

```text
Usage: edit [FILENAME]

Arguments:
  [FILENAME]
          File to edit (defaults to the active template)

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help
```

## `terms`

Spawn terminal screens onto the connected hosts.

```text
Usage: terms [OPTIONS] [TERMNAME]

Arguments:
  [TERMNAME]
          Terminal emulator script to spawn consoles with

Options:
  -t, --target <HOST>
          Host to act on. Can be used multiple times. If omitted all hosts are used

  -h, --help
          Print help
```
